overview.short: Thiruvananthapuram district (Malayalam: [t̪iɾuʋɐnɐn̪d̪ɐpuɾɐm] ) is the southernmost district in the Indian state of Kerala.
overview.description: Thiruvananthapuram district (Malayalam: [t̪iɾuʋɐnɐn̪d̪ɐpuɾɐm] ) is the southernmost district in the Indian state of Kerala. The district was created in 1949, with its headquarters in the city of Thiruvananthapuram, which is also Kerala's administrative centre. The present district was created in 1956 by separating the four southernmost Taluks of the erstwhile district to form Kanyakumari district. Trivandrum District, officially Thiruvananthapuram, is the southernmost district and capital of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Puthenthope
topPlaces.description: Puthenthope (Puthen - New, Thope - Orchard) is a coastal village in Thiruvananthapuram district in Kerala, India, and situated 19 km (12 mi) northwest of city centre, 16 km from Thiruvananthapuram International Airport. The village is mostly covered with coconut palms and cashew trees.

topPlaces.name: St Ignatius Church, Thiruvananthapuram
topPlaces.description: St Ignatius Church Puthenthope, Thiruvananthapuram, is a Catholic church located in the Kadinamkulam panchayat of Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, India. Puthenthope claims to have rich historical legacy.

topPlaces.name: St. Andrews, Kerala
topPlaces.description: St. Andrews is a seaside hamlet located in the city of Thiruvananthapuram, Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Vettuthura
topPlaces.description: Vettuthura is a suburb of Thiruvananthapuram, the capital of Kerala, India. The main job in this area is fishing.

topPlaces.name: Kaniyapuram
topPlaces.description: Kaniyapuram is a residential and IT industrial area of Thiruvananthapuram metropolitan area of Thiruvananthapuram city around 17 kilometres (11 mi) from Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, India. The main centre of Kaniyapuram area is located in NH-66 in between Kazhakkoottam and Pallippuram CRPF Base.

topPlaces.name: Pallithura
topPlaces.description: Pallithura is the north-west portion of Trivandrum city the capital of Kerala state in India. Literally translated from Malayalam, it means 'the beach (Thura) besides the church (Palli).

topPlaces.name: Pallippuram, Thiruvananthapuram
topPlaces.description: Pallippuram is a northern suburb of Thiruvananthapuram city of Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala. It is one of the fastest urbanising area of the Thiruvananthapuram UA.Technocity is situated in this area.

topPlaces.name: Kadinamkulam
topPlaces.description: Kadinamkulam is a Panchayat, Village and the northern suburb of Trivandrum City. It is one of the residential and industrial area of Trivandrum City in Kerala, India.

items.note: Puthenthope (Puthen - New, Thope - Orchard) is a coastal village in Thiruvananthapuram district in Kerala, India, and situated 19 km (12 mi) northwest of city centre, 16 km from Thiruvananthapuram International Airport. The village is mostly covered with coconut palms and cashew trees.
St Ignatius Church Puthenthope, Thiruvananthapuram, is a Catholic church located in the Kadinamkulam panchayat of Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, India. Puthenthope claims to have rich historical legacy.
St. Andrews is a seaside hamlet located in the city of Thiruvananthapuram, Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala.
Vettuthura is a suburb of Thiruvananthapuram, the capital of Kerala, India. The main job in this area is fishing.

Kaniyapuram is a residential and IT industrial area of Thiruvananthapuram metropolitan area of Thiruvananthapuram city around 17 kilometres (11 mi) from Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, India. The main centre of Kaniyapuram area is located in NH-66 in between Kazhakkoottam and Pallippuram CRPF Base.
Pallithura is the north-west portion of Trivandrum city the capital of Kerala state in India. Literally translated from Malayalam, it means 'the beach (Thura) besides the church (Palli).

Pallippuram is a northern suburb of Thiruvananthapuram city of Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala. It is one of the fastest urbanising area of the Thiruvananthapuram UA.Technocity is situated in this area.
Kadinamkulam is a Panchayat, Village and the northern suburb of Trivandrum City. It is one of the residential and industrial area of Trivandrum City in Kerala, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Puthenthope Beach?
faq.a: The best time to visit Puthenthope Beach is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Puthenthope Beach?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (14 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (16 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Puthenthope Beach?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Puthenthope Beach at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Puthenthope Beach?
faq.a: Stays in Puthenthope Beach start from ₹1,200 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Puthenthope Beach?
faq.a: The top attractions are Puthenthope, St Ignatius Church, Thiruvananthapuram, St. Andrews, Kerala, Vettuthura, Kaniyapuram.
